title.en: Passport Police Verification — Process, Adverse Reports and Fixing a Stuck Application

answerBox.en: After passport appointment formalities, the file goes for police verification: the local station confirms your address and checks criminal records — clear reports release the passport in days. Trouble comes from address mismatches, tenants without local proof, pending cases, and files sitting at the station. Fixes: watertight address documents, honest disclosure of any case (concealment is the real offence), status tracking, and the PSK grievance ladder.

en.title: Set the verification up to succeed
en.summary: Address documents that match, availability, local presence.
en.detail: PV checks two things: **you live where you claim**, and **criminal antecedents**. Give the address you actually live at (current address matters; you can hold mail elsewhere), with **matching proofs** (Aadhaar updated to it helps everything — [update first](/help/government-paperwork/aadhaar-card-corrections) if needed); tenants: rent agreement + landlord contactability. Keep your phone reachable — constables call before visiting; missed contact = "not found at address" reports. References/neighbours being aware you exist at the address genuinely matters in some states.

en.title: Pending or past cases: disclose, never conceal
en.summary: The form's criminal-history questions are the real test.
en.detail: Disclose FIRs/cases truthfully — **suppression is an offence under the Passports Act (Section 12)** and grounds for impounding later; disclosure, by contrast, routes you into a manageable process: pending-case applicants can get passports with **court permission** (a standard application in the pending case; courts grant travel-consistent permissions routinely) under the GSR exception framework, often as short-validity passports. Closed/acquitted cases: carry the final report/judgment copies for the verifying officer. An advocate's ₹299 consult before filing beats explanations after an adverse report.

en.title: Track and unstick the file
en.summary: mPassport status → station follow-up → PSK/SP escalation.
en.detail: Track on **[passportindia.gov.in](https://www.passportindia.gov.in)**/mPassport Seva ("police verification initiated/completed"). Sitting at the station beyond 2–4 weeks: visit politely with your documents (constables juggle piles; presence resolves most), then escalate — **grievance on the portal**, the **RPO's public grievance cell**, the district **SP's passport cell**, and CPGRAMS. States with **e-verification (mPassport Police app)** move in days; paper states need the follow-up. An [RTI](/help/government-paperwork/rti-application-how-to) asking the file's station-level status is the classic unsticker.

en.title: Adverse reports: contest with paper
en.summary: Representation to the RPO; annexures and affidavits cure most.
en.detail: Adverse PV ("not residing", "case pending", document doubts) triggers an RPO show-cause/rejection — respond with a **written representation + evidence**: fresh address proofs and neighbour/landlord affidavits for residence issues; court orders/final reports for case issues (our **₹199 annexure/affidavit service** prepares the standard formats). Re-verification is ordered on good representations. Refusals under Section 6 are appealable to the **Chief Passport Officer** (and writ jurisdiction beyond) — but the paper-first representation resolves the bulk without appeals.

rows.label: PV itself
rows.note: In passport fees; never pay constables

en.footnote: Bribe demands at verification are reportable (state anti-corruption helplines) — and unnecessary: clean files clear on their own, and stuck files respond to the escalation ladder above.

en: passport police verification me kitna time
passport verification stuck what to do

en.q: How long does police verification actually take?
en.a: App-based e-verification states: days. Paper-file states: 1–3 weeks typically, plus RPO processing. The overall passport timeline is dominated by PV — which is why address-document hygiene and reachability are the real speed levers.

en.q: I'm a tenant and the landlord won't give documents. What works?
en.a: A registered rent agreement is the cleanest; failing that, UIDAI-updated Aadhaar at the address plus bank/utility mail there builds the case, and a landlord's simple consent letter usually suffices where full documents are refused. Chronic mismatch cases should update Aadhaar first — it anchors every later verification.

en.q: There's an old closed FIR against me. Will my passport be refused?
en.a: Closed/acquitted matters are not refusal grounds — but disclose them and carry the closure/acquittal papers for the verifying officer and RPO. Refusals and impoundings arise from PENDING proceedings without court permission, and from concealment. Honest disclosure + documents = manageable.

en.q: A criminal case is pending against me. Can I get a passport at all?
en.a: Yes — with the criminal court's permission: a standard application in your pending case seeking leave to obtain/renew a passport (and travel, if needed). Courts grant these routinely for work/family travel with conditions. The GSR framework then lets the RPO issue, commonly a short-validity passport during the case.

en.q: The constable demanded money to file a "good" report. What should I do?
en.a: You are not required to pay anything — PV is part of your paid passport service. Note the identifiers, report on the state anti-corruption/vigilance helpline and to the SP's office, and continue the normal process; files clear on documents. Paying invites repeat demands and validates the racket.

reviewer: Adv. Rohan Mehta, Bar Council of Maharashtra & Goa, MAH/2214/2011
